input.feature: Real-time notification system for a project management SaaS
input.context: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL, React frontend, 5K DAU
input.requirements: Sub-second delivery, read receipts, per-user preferences

problem_statement.problem: Users miss important project updates because notifications are email-only with 5-15 minute delay
problem_statement.affected: All active users (5K DAU), especially team leads managing multiple projects
problem_statement.current_behavior: Email notifications on task assignment and due date, batched every 15 minutes
problem_statement.desired_behavior: Real-time in-app notifications with sub-second delivery, plus configurable email digest
problem_statement.success_criteria: 95% of notifications delivered in < 1s, 30% reduction in missed deadlines

solution_design.approach: WebSocket-based notification system with PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY for fan-out and Redis for presence tracking. Client connects via WebSocket on page load, receives typed notification events.

alternatives_rejected.reason: Too much server load at scale, not truly real-time
External dependency, data leaves our infrastructure

decisions.trade_off: More complex but bidirectional (needed for read receipts)
decisions.revisit_if: If mobile needs push, SSE might be simpler

decisions.trade_off: Fewer moving parts but 1K/s throughput ceiling

edge_cases.handling: All tabs receive via shared WebSocket, read sync via broadcast
Client auto-reconnects with last_seen_id, server replays missed notifications
Group by type, show '12 new task assignments' instead of 12 individual

edge_cases.case: User preference update during active session
edge_cases.handling: Preference change takes effect immediately via WebSocket config update
Fallback polling every 30s as safety net, log if triggered

implementation_plan.deliverable: User can configure and mark read
Wired into task/comment/deadline events, load tested
